rooms.name: Altar
rooms.description: A sacred shrine or holy place dedicated to a deity

rooms.name: Alchemy Lab
rooms.description: Workstation for crafting alchemical items

rooms.name: Armory
rooms.description: Racks of weapons and armor for guards or militia

rooms.name: Ballroom
rooms.description: Grand hall for dances and formal social events

rooms.name: Bar
rooms.description: A counter with stools for serving drinks

rooms.name: Bath
rooms.description: Tubs, water heating, and bathing supplies

rooms.name: Bedroom
rooms.description: Furnished sleeping quarters

rooms.name: Book Repository
rooms.description: Library shelves stocked with books and scrolls

rooms.name: Brewery
rooms.description: Kettles, fermentation vats, and storage barrels

rooms.name: Bunks
rooms.description: Simple shared sleeping quarters for workers or troops

rooms.name: Cell
rooms.description: Barred room for holding prisoners

rooms.name: Classroom
rooms.description: Desks and a lectern for teaching students

rooms.name: Common Room
rooms.description: Large open room for socializing, eating, and relaxing

rooms.name: Courtyard
rooms.description: Open-air area surrounded by building walls

rooms.name: Crypt
rooms.description: Underground burial vault, often beneath a temple

rooms.name: Dojo
rooms.description: Training hall for martial practice

rooms.name: Escape Route
rooms.description: Hidden tunnel or passage for quick escape

rooms.name: False Front
rooms.description: A facade that hides the building's true purpose

rooms.name: Forge
rooms.description: Anvil, bellows, and furnace for metalwork

rooms.name: Garden
rooms.description: Cultivated growing area for herbs or vegetables

rooms.name: Gatehouse
rooms.description: Fortified entry point with heavy doors and murder holes

rooms.name: Habitat
rooms.description: Enclosure for keeping exotic or trained animals

rooms.name: Hatchery
rooms.description: Incubation area for eggs and young creatures

rooms.name: Infirmary
rooms.description: Cots and medical supplies for treating the injured

rooms.name: Kitchen
rooms.description: Hearth, prep tables, and cooking supplies

rooms.name: Laundry
rooms.description: Washtubs and drying lines for cleaning linens

rooms.name: Lavatory
rooms.description: Basic sanitary facilities

rooms.name: Lodging
rooms.description: Comfortable guest quarters for travelers

rooms.name: Office
rooms.description: Desk, chair, filing for administrative work

rooms.name: Sanctum
rooms.description: Private magical workroom warded for concentration

rooms.name: Sewing Room
rooms.description: Looms and fabric for making garments and textiles

rooms.name: Shop Front
rooms.description: Counter and display area for selling wares

rooms.name: Sitting Room
rooms.description: Comfortable chairs for private meetings or relaxation

rooms.name: Stable
rooms.description: Stalls, feed storage, and water troughs for horses

rooms.name: Stage
rooms.description: Raised platform for performances and speeches

rooms.name: Statue
rooms.description: Decorative stone or metal sculpture

rooms.name: Storage
rooms.description: Shelved room for storing goods and supplies

rooms.name: Throne Room
rooms.description: Grand audience chamber with a seat of power

rooms.name: Tower
rooms.description: Tall fortified structure for defense and observation

rooms.name: Trap
rooms.description: Concealed mechanical or magical hazard protecting an area

rooms.name: Vault
rooms.description: Reinforced room with heavy lock for storing valuables

rooms.name: War Room
rooms.description: Strategic planning room with maps and battle models

rooms.name: Workstation
rooms.description: General-purpose crafting area for artisan work

teams.name: Acolytes
teams.description: Novice clergy providing minor magical services

teams.name: Apprentices
teams.description: Students learning arcane or craft skills

teams.name: Bureaucrats
teams.description: Administrators and clerks who manage affairs

teams.name: Drivers
teams.description: Wagon and cart operators for transport

teams.name: Laborers
teams.description: Unskilled workers for heavy lifting and basic tasks

teams.name: Lackeys
teams.description: Servants and attendants for daily needs

teams.name: Robbers
teams.description: Thieves and thugs for illicit operations

teams.name: Sage
teams.description: Scholar with deep knowledge in a field

teams.name: Scofflaws
teams.description: Rogues skilled in evading law and gathering intel

buildings.name: Academy
buildings.benefit: +2 on Knowledge checks when researching in your academy

buildings.name: Alchemist
buildings.benefit: Craft alchemical items at double speed

buildings.name: Arena
buildings.benefit: Hold gladiatorial contests that generate influence

buildings.name: Bar
buildings.benefit: +2 on Diplomacy and Gather Information in your bar

buildings.name: Castle
buildings.benefit: Seat of power granting +4 on Leadership score

buildings.name: Cathedral
buildings.benefit: Access to divine spellcasting services up to 5th level

buildings.name: Guildhall
buildings.benefit: +2 on Craft/Profession checks for guild members

buildings.name: Herbalist
buildings.benefit: Purchase potions and herbal remedies at 10% discount

buildings.name: Hospital
buildings.benefit: Party members heal 2 extra HP per day of rest here

buildings.name: Inn
buildings.benefit: Comfortable lodging; +2 circumstance on rest recovery

buildings.name: Library
buildings.benefit: +4 on Knowledge checks when you have access to your library

buildings.name: Mansion
buildings.benefit: Luxurious personal estate impressing visitors

buildings.name: Monastery
buildings.benefit: Characters training here gain a bonus to Wis-based skill checks

buildings.name: Shop
buildings.benefit: Generates income from selling general merchandise

buildings.name: Smithy
buildings.benefit: Craft weapons and armor at double speed

buildings.name: Tavern
buildings.benefit: +2 on Diplomacy and Gather Information; rumors and contacts

buildings.name: Temple
buildings.benefit: Access to divine spellcasting services up to 3rd level

buildings.name: Thieves' Guild
buildings.benefit: +2 on Sleight of Hand and Disable Device in your territory

buildings.name: Watch Tower
buildings.benefit: Early warning of approaching threats within 5 miles

buildings.name: Wizard's Tower
buildings.benefit: Access to arcane spellcasting services up to 5th level

events.name: Good Fortune
events.effect: One of your buildings earns double capital for 1d6 days

events.name: Visiting Merchant
events.effect: A traveling merchant offers rare goods at 10% below market price for 1d4 days

events.name: New Contacts
events.effect: Gain 1d4 points of Influence from making useful connections

events.name: Famous Visitor
events.effect: A notable figure visits your establishment, granting 1d6 Influence

events.name: Pilgrimage
events.effect: Religious pilgrims boost your Influence and Magic by 1d4 each

events.name: Local Festival
events.effect: A festival near your business increases all capital earned by 50% for 1d3 days

events.name: Uneventful
events.effect: Nothing unusual happens; business proceeds normally

events.name: Rumor
events.effect: An interesting but unconfirmed rumor reaches you — could lead to a quest hook

events.name: Tax Increase
events.effect: Local taxes increase; lose 1d6 x 10 gp or 1d4 Goods

events.name: Vandalism
events.effect: Hooligans damage a room in one building; costs 1d4 Goods and 1d4 Labor to repair

events.name: Theft
events.effect: Thieves steal 2d6 x 10 gp worth of goods from your building

events.name: Fire
events.effect: Fire damages a building; one room is destroyed unless you spend 2d6 x 10 gp for repairs

events.name: Labor Dispute
events.effect: Workers demand higher pay; lose 1d4 Influence and 1d4 Labor, or pay 1d6 x 20 gp

events.name: Infestation
events.effect: Pests or monsters infest a building; must clear them (mini-encounter) or lose 1d4 Goods/day

events.name: Rival Business
events.effect: A competitor opens nearby; all buildings earn half capital for 2d6 days unless you act

economy.name: Economy
economy.description: Measures trade, industry, and the kingdom's wealth

loyalty.name: Loyalty
loyalty.description: Measures the trust and devotion of the kingdom's citizens

stability.name: Stability
stability.description: Measures the physical and social health of the kingdom

leadershipRoles.name: Ruler
leadershipRoles.vacancy: -4 to all, cannot claim hexes, create armies or build

leadershipRoles.name: Councilor
leadershipRoles.vacancy: Loyalty -2, cannot gain benefits from festivals

leadershipRoles.name: High Priest
leadershipRoles.vacancy: Stability and Loyalty -2, cannot build religious buildings

leadershipRoles.name: Royal Enforcer
leadershipRoles.vacancy: None (optional), but Loyalty -2 if filled then vacated

terrainImprovements.name: Aqueduct
terrainImprovements.bonus: Loyalty +1, Stability +1 per hex with Aqueduct

terrainImprovements.name: Road
terrainImprovements.bonus: Economy +1 per 4 road hexes; reduces travel time

buildings.name: Arena
buildings.special: Halves Unrest increase from continuous events

buildings.name: Caster's Tower
buildings.special: Provides magic item slot for 3rd+ level items

buildings.name: Exotic Artisan
buildings.special: City base value +1000 gp for magic items

buildings.name: Magic Shop
buildings.special: City base value +2000 gp for magic items

buildings.name: Pier
buildings.special: City base value +1000 gp, must border water

buildings.name: Waterfront
buildings.special: City base value +4000 gp, must border water

buildings.name: Waterway
buildings.special: Must connect to river or canal

kingdomEvents.name: Natural Blessing
kingdomEvents.effect: Economy +2 and Stability +2 for one turn

kingdomEvents.name: Discovery
kingdomEvents.effect: Explorers find a valuable resource; gain 1d6 BP

kingdomEvents.name: Diplomatic Overture
kingdomEvents.effect: A neighboring realm offers alliance; Loyalty +2 for 1d6 turns

kingdomEvents.name: Bumper Crop
kingdomEvents.effect: Farms produce extra; Consumption reduced by 50% for one turn

kingdomEvents.name: Boomtown
kingdomEvents.effect: One city's economy booms; +1d4 BP this turn

kingdomEvents.name: No Event
kingdomEvents.effect: The kingdom runs smoothly this turn

kingdomEvents.name: Food Shortage
kingdomEvents.effect: Consumption increases by 50% for 1d4 turns. Stability check DC 15 or +1 Unrest

kingdomEvents.name: Plague
kingdomEvents.effect: Stability -4 until a Stability check DC 20 succeeds. +1d4 Unrest

kingdomEvents.name: Bandits
kingdomEvents.effect: Bandits raid the countryside. Lose 1d4 BP. Stability check DC 15 or +1 Unrest

kingdomEvents.name: Monster Attack
kingdomEvents.effect: A dangerous creature threatens a hex. Must be defeated or lose the hex improvement

kingdomEvents.name: Feud
kingdomEvents.effect: Factions within the kingdom clash. Loyalty -2 until resolved (DC 18 Loyalty check)

kingdomEvents.name: Smugglers
kingdomEvents.effect: Economy -2 for 1d4 turns. Spymaster can attempt to root them out (DC 16)

kingdomEvents.name: Squatters
kingdomEvents.effect: Settlers claim unclaimed land. Loyalty check DC 15 or lose 1d4 BP settling dispute

kingdomEvents.name: Assassination Attempt
kingdomEvents.effect: An attempt on a leader's life. Succeed at Stability DC 20 or the leader is injured (out 1d4 turns)

kingdomEvents.name: Rebellion
kingdomEvents.effect: Citizens revolt. +1d6 Unrest. Loyalty check DC 22 or a city is damaged (random building destroyed)

kingdomEvents.name: Invasion
kingdomEvents.effect: An army marches on your kingdom. Must be defeated in mass combat or lose 1d3 hexes

types.name: Ability Score Increase
types.description: Retrain an ability score increase gained from leveling at 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th, or 20th level

types.name: Archetype
types.description: Replace current archetype features with another compatible archetype

types.name: Class Feature
types.description: Swap one class feature for another available at the same level

types.name: Class Level
types.description: Change most recent class level to another class. Must meet prerequisites

types.name: Feat
types.description: Replace one feat with another for which you qualify

types.name: Hit Points
types.description: Reroll HP for one level (keep result even if lower)

types.name: New Language
types.description: Learn a new language by studying with a speaker

types.name: Racial Trait
types.description: Swap one racial trait for an alternate racial trait

types.name: Skill Ranks
types.description: Reassign skill ranks from one skill to another (must be class skill or have ranks)

types.name: Spell Known
types.description: Swap a spell known for another spell of the same level on your class list

trainers.description: You must have a trainer who has the feature you're retraining to. The trainer charges the listed cost per day.

types.name: Criminal Enterprise
types.description: Thieves' guild, smuggling ring, or similar

types.name: Political Faction
types.description: Bloc of influence within a settlement

loyaltyEvents.name: Betrayal
loyaltyEvents.effect: A key member defects; lose 1d4 Influence and 1d4 members

loyaltyEvents.name: Internal Strife
loyaltyEvents.effect: Factional dispute; Loyalty check DC 15 or lose 1 Loyalty

loyaltyEvents.name: Good Month
loyaltyEvents.effect: Extra earnings: roll earnings dice twice, keep higher

loyaltyEvents.name: New Recruit
loyaltyEvents.effect: A skilled individual seeks to join your organization

loyaltyEvents.name: Windfall
loyaltyEvents.effect: Unexpected opportunity; earn triple normal earnings this period

storyFeats.name: Accursed
storyFeats.prerequisite: You must carry a curse that can be broken
storyFeats.benefit: You gain +2 on saves vs curse effects and +1 on attack rolls vs the source of your curse
storyFeats.completion: The save bonus becomes +4 and you gain +2 on all saves for 24 hours after breaking the curse

storyFeats.name: Champion
storyFeats.prerequisite: You must have defeated a challenging foe in single combat
storyFeats.benefit: +1 on attack rolls and +1 dodge AC when fighting a single opponent
storyFeats.goal: Defeat a CR equal to your level+2 enemy in single combat
storyFeats.completion: The bonuses increase to +2 and you gain the Diehard feat

storyFeats.name: Damned
storyFeats.prerequisite: You must have made a bargain with an evil outsider
storyFeats.benefit: +2 on Diplomacy and Intimidate vs evil outsiders
storyFeats.goal: Break free from your dark bargain
storyFeats.completion: You gain SR equal to 11 + level vs evil spells

storyFeats.name: Feral Heart
storyFeats.prerequisite: You must have survived alone in the wilderness for a month or more
storyFeats.benefit: +2 on Survival and Handle Animal checks
storyFeats.goal: Tame or befriend a wild animal companion of CR 4+
storyFeats.completion: You gain a +4 bonus on wild empathy checks and can communicate with animals at will

storyFeats.name: Forgotten Past
storyFeats.prerequisite: You have amnesia or a significant gap in your memory
storyFeats.benefit: +2 on Will saves vs mind-affecting effects
storyFeats.completion: You gain a permanent +2 insight bonus on all Knowledge checks

storyFeats.name: Liberator
storyFeats.prerequisite: You must have been imprisoned or enslaved, or witnessed such injustice
storyFeats.benefit: +1 on attack and damage rolls against slavers and jailers
storyFeats.goal: Free a significant number of captives (20+) or overthrow a tyrant
storyFeats.completion: Bonuses increase to +2 and you gain Freedom of Movement 1/day as a spell-like ability

storyFeats.name: Lost Legacy
storyFeats.prerequisite: Your family lost something important (heirloom, title, land)
storyFeats.benefit: +1 on Diplomacy and Knowledge (history) checks relating to your legacy
storyFeats.goal: Recover or restore your family's lost legacy
storyFeats.completion: You gain a +2 bonus on Leadership score and a significant material reward

storyFeats.name: Nemesis
storyFeats.prerequisite: You have a recurring personal enemy
storyFeats.benefit: +1 on attack rolls, saves, and skill checks against your nemesis
storyFeats.completion: All bonuses increase to +2 and you gain 1 bonus feat

storyFeats.name: Prophesied
storyFeats.prerequisite: A prophecy exists about your destiny
storyFeats.benefit: Once per day, reroll a single d20 roll (must take second result)
storyFeats.completion: The reroll becomes 2/day, and you gain a +2 luck bonus on the rerolled check

storyFeats.name: Shamed
storyFeats.prerequisite: You have been publicly disgraced or dishonored
storyFeats.benefit: +2 on saves vs fear effects and +1 on Intimidate
storyFeats.goal: Restore your honor through a significant public deed
storyFeats.completion: You gain +4 on Diplomacy in the region where you were shamed, and the fear save bonus becomes +4
